title: rubus pubescens subsp. flaccidus
intro: Rubus pubescens subsp. flaccidus, also known as the creeping raspberry, is a subspecies of the raspberry plant that is native to North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus pubescens subsp. flaccidus include reducing inflammation and improving digestive health, as well as having antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage. Rubus pubescens subsp. flaccidus can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
